summary:
Russell's bodies appear within plasma cells as a result of undegraded immunoglobulin-heavy chains; they do not form in other cell types like activated macrophages, histiocytes, or erythrocytes. \(\boxed{\text{Plasma cells}}\) Summary:
Russell's bodies consist of unprocessed immunoglobulin-heavy chains accumulated inside plasma cells but not inactivated immune cells such as macrophages or red blood cells.\(\boxed{\text{Plasma cells}}\)

Russell's bodies consist of unprocessed immunoglobulin-heavy chains accumulated inside plasma cells but not inactivated immune cells such as macrophages or red blood cells.\(\boxed{\text{Plasma cells}}\). Answer choice: C
